findings.issue: AppRouter.tsx imports from `@/pkce` twice, which creates noisy imports and is unnecessary.
findings.remediation: Combine both `@/pkce` imports into a single statement: `import { retrieveVerifier, clearVerifier, clearInvitationToken, generateVerifier, generateChallenge, storeVerifier } from '@/pkce'`.

findings.issue: `makeRequest` always adds a stored `access_token` Authorization header unless the caller provides one. Because `oauthApi.init` and `oauthApi.redeem` use `makeRequest`, the unauthenticated OAuth endpoints can receive a stale or unrelated Bearer token.
findings.remediation: Provide a way to opt out of the auto-added Authorization header (e.g., a special header or an option), and use it in `oauthApi.init`/`redeem` so OAuth handshake requests are sent without credentials.

findings.issue: `makeRequest` adds a per-request `abort` listener to the caller's AbortSignal via `anySignal`. If the caller signal is long-lived or slow to abort, listeners and controller references accumulate until the signal fires.
findings.remediation: Use `AbortSignal.any` where available and/or remove the listener after the fetch settles to avoid leaking controllers/listeners on long-lived signals.

findings.issue: `generateVerifier` relies on an unqualified `crypto` global, while the SHA-256 fallback consistently uses `globalThis.crypto`. In environments where the lexical `crypto` binding is missing or shadowed (some test runners, workers), verifier generation will throw before the fallback can run.
findings.remediation: Use `globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues(array)` so verifier generation uses the same robust global as the challenge fallback.

findings.issue: `InvitationPage` builds the return-to URL for OAuth by interpolating `token` directly, without URL-encoding it. A pathological invitation token with reserved characters could corrupt the callback URL structure.
findings.remediation: Encode the token when constructing the URL: ``/invitations/${encodeURIComponent(token)}/complete``.

findings.issue: `OAuthCallbackPage` guards against an aborted signal before persisting the access token but not before calling `setIsRedirecting(true)` immediately afterward. If the component unmounts between redeem and that state update, React may log a state-update-after-unmount warning.
findings.remediation: Check `abortController.signal.aborted` again right before `setIsRedirecting(true)` (or combine the token persistence and redirect in a single guarded block).
